description: The Bandidos Motorcycle Club is an outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1966 in San Leon, Texas by Donald Chambers. With an estimated 3,000-3,500 members across 303 chapters in 22 countries, it is the fourth-largest motorcycle club globally. Classified by law enforcement as an organized crime syndicate, the club has been involved in drug trafficking, violent feuds with rival clubs, and internal conflicts.

country_links.evidence_quote: the first foreign chapter was established in Sydney , Australia in 1983.
country_links.context: The Bandidos established their first foreign chapter in Sydney, Australia in 1983.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Bandidos embarked on a failed endeavour to establish themselves in Canada after merging with the Montreal -based Rock Machine Motorcycle Club in 2000.
country_links.context: The Bandidos merged with the Rock Machine Motorcycle Club in Canada in 2000 and established chapters there.

country_links.evidence_quote: and on the Channel Islands in 2003.
country_links.context: The Bandidos founded a chapter on the Channel Islands in 2003.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2004, the Bandidos formed a chapter in Costa Rica .
country_links.context: The Bandidos formed a chapter in Costa Rica in 2004.

country_links.evidence_quote: branches being established in Denmark in 1993
country_links.context: The Bandidos established a branch in Denmark in 1993.
The Bandidos established a branch in Finland in 1995.

country_links.context: The Bandidos formed a chapter in Marseille, France in 1989.

country_links.evidence_quote: The German department of the Bandidos was chartered in 2000.
country_links.context: The Bandidos chartered a department in Germany in 2000.

country_links.evidence_quote: The club further expanded to Malaysia and Singapore in 2006.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Bandidos formed its first chapter in the Netherlands in 2014.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Bandidos have a small but growing presence in New Zealand after a rocky start in 2012. They claim to have more than a dozen patched members and prospects in the Christchurch area.
country_links.context: The Bandidos have a growing presence in New Zealand with members in Christchurch.
The Bandidos established a branch in Norway in 1995.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2001, the Bandidos were established in Thailand via a merger with the Diablos Motorcycle Club in Pattaya .
country_links.context: The Bandidos were established in Thailand via a merger in 2001.
